description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a wooden superstructure. The well is topped with a pitched, shingled roof supported by two sturdy wooden posts. A horizontal wooden axle runs between the posts, equipped with a hand crank and a rope mechanism for drawing water. A wooden bucket is attached to the rope, hanging over the open well. The stonework is rugged and dark, while the wood appears weathered, giving the well an aged, rustic appearance. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, suitable for outdoor urban or park settings.
description: A rustic stone water well with a wooden roof, hand crank, and bucket, featuring weathered wood and rugged stonework.
description_view_0: Front view showing the hand crank, bucket, and full roof structure.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular roof profile and wooden support beams.
description_view_2: Rear view with the bucket and rope mechanism visible, showing the back of the roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the wooden support and crank handle.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A wooden bucket attached to a rope and hand crank mechanism is present, suspended over the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high surrounds the well; wooden posts and a pitched roof provide additional protection.
